outcomes.note: No match played since Run 5. Final: Spain v Argentina, Jul 19, MetLife, 3 p.m. ET. Grading of Run 1-5 scorecards belongs to the score step.

carriedForwardFromRun5: All Run 1-5 verified facts stand: Spain 2-0 France with the disputed Digne-on-Yamal penalty; England 1-2 Argentina (Gordon 55'; E. Fernandez 85', L. Martinez 90+2' from a Messi cross; ARG 64% possession, xG ~1.84 v ~0.53; referee Elfath, no controversy of substance); market flip to Spain; Opta derived .569/.431 with publishedPostSF1 provenance; Silver paywall note; five named banks; Miami Herald ~$90M; La Nacion $260M/$57M attribution; Tofoni ~3h videoconference interview (witness, not charged); Tapia travel-authorization/travel-bond nugget; AFA pushback quote; no US charges; FBI declined comment; no suspensions for the final; author ranges ESP 55-60 / ARG 40-45.
newSinceRun5: FINANCIAL THREAD MOVED (biggest item): La Nacion (Jul 13-14) reports the FBI/prosecutors reconstructing the scheme 'from the inside' via former AFA insiders, not just outside witnesses. A former executive president of the AFA Consejo Federal (a Toviggino lieutenant) has reportedly held several INFORMAL videoconferences with US authorities - explicitly not sworn testimony and not yet a formal cooperating witness. Three federal prosecutors named across DC and SDFL. New alleged mechanism: cash via 'cuevas'. AFA sources now cast Toviggino, not Tapia, as principal target. Still NO charges/subpoena/indictment; FBI declines comment. H2 bands HELD (changeMind trigger unmet); added timeline node fbi-from-inside, H2 forPoint, source lanacion-fbi-inside.
Separate Argentine DOMESTIC 'mansion' case (distinct from the ~$12.7M tax matter) advanced to a jurisdiction fight; hearing set Aug 12, 2026. Noted lightly.

PERCEPTION NARRATIVE CRESTED: a multi-million-signature petition demanding Argentina's expulsion (centered on the Egypt R16). Rebutted on the record - Collina ('unfounded allegations have no place in our sport'; FIFA refereeing not influenced by anyone 'not even by the FIFA president'), Scaloni (social-media amplification), and analysts noting England/Germany/Iran drew contentious VAR calls too. Added timeline node favoritism-petition, H4 forPoint, sources forbes-favoritism + football365-favoritism. H4 band HELD (outstanding test is the final).

FINAL REFEREE NOT YET APPOINTED at pass time. Matchup narrows the neutral field (no UEFA, no CONMEBOL): frontrunner Alireza Faghani (Iran/AFC). English refs (Oliver, Taylor) barred over Falklands; Wilton Sampaio (Brazil) ruled out; French crew (Letexier, Turpin) sent home by routine protocol. Structural point cutting against H1 (final officials confederation-neutral by rule). No timeline node for a non-appointment; Run 7 records the name.

MARKET drifted slightly toward Spain, still inside the range: Kalshi ~57.7 -> ~58.5, Polymarket ~58.4; DraftKings 90-min Spain +125 -> +115 (ARG +260 -> +285). Named trophy lines uncoupled/carried; ~59/41 normalized. BOOKMAKERS.asOf -> 2026-07-16. AUTHOR_RANGES unchanged.
OPTA re-checked Jul 16: still no explicit final pair (public model still frames a Spain-v-England final). Derived .569/.431 stands. Silver still paywalled.

SQUAD news: Spain's Yamal (left-thigh strapping) and Porro (hamstring/fatigue, not serious) trained apart Thursday Jul 16 - expected fit for Sunday. Argentina clean (Romero available, Paredes no concern). No suspensions either side. Predicted XIs are previews, not confirmed - not adopted.

BUILDUP (Log color): Messi (39), likely his last WC match, 8 goals tied for the lead, vs 19-year-old Yamal in their first-ever meeting; Trump to attend and present the trophy; unprecedented WC-final halftime show; ~60% shower chance with a thunderstorm/lightning DELAY risk July 19 in NJ. Attendance not officially released - omitted.
